title: Microkernel Core epic
summary: Implement and assure the native Agent OS microkernel and architecture ports.

title: Freeze executable kernel semantics v0
summary: Translate kernel object, authority, lifecycle, error, concurrency, and resource rules into executable reference models and tests.

title: Implement physical memory manager
summary: Track usable, reserved, device, firmware, secure, persistent, and crash memory with bounded allocation behavior.

title: Implement virtual address spaces and mappings
summary: Create architecture-independent address-space objects with explicit mapping rights, ownership, invalidation, and teardown.

title: Enter user mode and launch initial process
summary: Load a signed/test user image, create its address space and handles, enter user mode, and receive syscalls/exceptions safely.

title: Implement kernel object and lifetime framework
summary: Provide typed reference-counted kernel objects with stable IDs, rights checks, wait signals, destruction, and diagnostic state.

title: Implement process and thread lifecycle
summary: Create, start, stop, kill, wait, inspect, and reap processes/threads with bounded authority and resources.

title: Prove user-process isolation and fault containment
summary: Demonstrate that memory, illegal instruction, syscall, resource, and crash faults remain within defined process/job boundaries.

title: Implement monotonic time, deadlines, and timer objects
summary: Expose monotonic clocks, bounded deadlines, cancellation, timer objects, and time-source quality to user space.

title: Implement baseline scheduler
summary: Schedule runnable threads with bounded classes, fairness, affinity, accounting, preemption, and idle behavior.

title: Implement synchronization primitives and futex-like wait
summary: Provide minimal kernel-assisted blocking for user-space mutexes/conditions with cancellation and priority policy.

title: Implement capability spaces and handle operations
summary: Represent explicit per-process authority with typed handles, rights, duplication, transfer preparation, close, and introspection.

title: Implement synchronous IPC channel and message transfer
summary: Transfer bounded typed bytes and capabilities between endpoints with clear ownership, cancellation, timeout, and peer-close behavior.

title: Implement wait sets and asynchronous notification objects
summary: Allow services to wait on bounded sets of signals, interrupts, channels, timers, and lifecycle events without polling.

title: Implement jobs, quotas, and resource accounting
summary: Bound processes and services by memory, handles, threads, IPC, CPU time and kernel-resource budgets.

title: Enable symmetric multiprocessing and cross-CPU coordination
summary: Run processes across multiple CPUs with correct interrupt routing, scheduling, TLB invalidation, object synchronization, and hot/offline policy.

title: Validate IPC priority propagation and deadline behavior
summary: Prevent unbounded priority inversion and characterize IPC/scheduler latency under adversarial service graphs.

title: Implement IOMMU and DMA ownership primitives
summary: Give user-space driver domains safe, bounded DMA mappings and interrupt resources without general physical-memory authority.

title: Implement structured crash records and kernel diagnostics
summary: Produce bounded, privacy-aware crash evidence sufficient for remote debugging without requiring a live debugger.

title: Fuzz and property-test kernel syscall surface
summary: Continuously exercise syscall decoding, object lifecycle, IPC, memory mappings, handles, cancellation and quotas against executable models.

title: Freeze kernel API level 0 at capability vertical-slice gate
summary: Approve the first coherent experimental kernel ABI only after object, IPC, resource, failure, and versioning semantics pass G2.

title: Portable Platform epic
summary: Deliver IDL, board packages, user-space drivers, services, SDK, and conformance.

title: Implement user-space service manager
summary: Start, stop, restart, monitor, route capabilities to, and update isolated services from a declarative manifest.

title: Implement structured logging and diagnostics service
summary: Collect bounded structured logs, metrics, traces, crash references, target identity, and privacy classes through explicit capabilities.

title: Demonstrate a native API application slice without POSIX
summary: Run a small user-space shell, storage object, timer, logging, and message workflow using only Agent OS syscalls/IDL/capabilities.

title: Implement time, entropy, and identity foundation services
summary: Expose wall-time synchronization, monotonic mapping, entropy, device/user/service identity references, and trust quality through separate services.

title: Implement hardware resource broker
summary: Grant scoped MMIO, interrupt, DMA, clock/reset/power and bus resources to isolated driver domains from board packages.

title: Implement bus discovery and device binding framework
summary: Discover/bind platform, PCI, USB and simple serial-bus devices without embedding one OS device model in native contracts.

title: Implement interrupt and DMA broker integration
summary: Connect user-space driver event loops to kernel interrupt/DMA primitives with safe buffer ownership and backpressure.

title: Demonstrate driver-domain crash and restart recovery
summary: Restart an isolated device driver and rebind clients without restarting kernel or corrupting canonical state.

title: Implement durable object/filesystem service v0
summary: Provide transactional namespaces, objects/files, metadata, quotas, snapshots, integrity checks and recovery for system/product storage.

title: Implement native network service v0
summary: Provide interface/link/IP/transport/DNS/connectivity contracts with per-component network capabilities, accounting, and test backends.

title: Implement USB host/device service baseline
summary: Enumerate USB, isolate class/function drivers, transfer buffers safely, handle hotplug/reset, and support debug/recovery needs.

title: Implement input service and semantic event contract
summary: Normalize touch, pointer, keyboard, switch, stylus and sensors into secure timestamped input streams and semantic focus actions.

title: Implement audio service and graph contract
summary: Route capture/playback streams, devices, volume/policy, timing, privacy indicators, low-latency paths and telephony endpoints through native services.

title: Specify GPU acceleration and command-submission contract
summary: Separate portable graphics APIs, GPU memory/context/queue services, compiler/runtime backends, and device-specific command streams.
